reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own answer to 72 ÷ 9 is certain, and in all previous games every player has correctly solved similarly easy arithmetic, with no conflicting capsules ever appearing. That makes it overwhelmingly likely that Q2-B, Q3-C, and Q4-D are also correct. Verifying would cost coins and still risk a random death if no one volunteers, as happened in Game 1, while adding almost no safety for such simple puzzles. By volunteering now with all single-labeled capsules, I maximize my survival chance and keep all 4 coins if (as is very likely) the password is correct.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q3  
REASONING: In Round 1 every capsule (Q1–Q4) has only a single endorsement, so volunteering now would mean staking my life on three completely unverified answers from A, C, and D. If nobody volunteers, the random-death risk (1/4) is the same for me whether I pass or verify, so it’s better to spend 1 coin to improve our information for safer decisions in later rounds. I’ll verify Q3 (C’s puzzle) rather than my own, since I already know my answer, and unchecked middle questions like Q3 have caused failures in a previous game.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q4  
REASONING: I’m confident in my own Q3 answer but have no information yet about Q1–Q2–Q4. Historically, Q4 has been the shakiest (wrong once, right twice), so it’s the highest-priority one to double-check. If no one volunteers this round, a random player dies whether I pass or verify, so my death risk this round is the same either way; spending 1 coin to verify Q4 just buys valuable information for safer volunteering later.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
🔍 Verifications completed:
  ✅ B agrees with Q3-C → now Q3-C+B (-1 coin: 4→3)
  ✅ C agrees with Q4-D → now Q4-D+C (-1 coin: 4→3)

✅ A entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C+B, Q4-D+C
🎉 Everyone escapes! 🎉
